us.reqs: No visa or residency action required.
us.strategy: Stay where you are. Healthcare via ACA pre-65, Medicare 65+. The simplest path on paper, but the most expensive on cost.
us.caveats: Family/lifestyle stays familiar. Highest annual spend ($96k) is the trade-off.

taiwan.tier: No visa needed · NHI from day one
taiwan.reqs: Boss holds Taiwanese citizenship. No visa, no Gold Card, no APRC needed. Just register household and request NHI. The 6-month residency rule for returning citizens to access NHI applies, but no other visa friction.
taiwan.pr_pathway: Already at home. Taiwan ID enables NHI, real estate, banking, voting — full citizenship rights.
taiwan.strategy: Taiwan is the simplest landing zone in the lineup. Boss can move whenever. Mandarin native. Family network. NHI provides world-class healthcare at ~$30/mo per person. No tax-residency complications until 183+ days/yr (worldwide income tax kicks in then — careful structuring on US-source investments needed via TW CPA).
taiwan.caveats: Worldwide income tax for TW tax residents (5-40% progressive) once 183+ days/yr. Foreign income under NT$1M (~$33k) per household exempt. US-Taiwan tax treaty benefits apply.

thailand.reqs: Wealthy Pensioner track (age 50+): $80k/yr passive income OR $40k/yr passive + $250k Thai investment. Wealthy Global Citizen track: $1M global net assets (income req REMOVED Feb 2025). Boss qualifies for either at FIRE.
thailand.pr_pathway: No direct PR via LTR. Renewable indefinitely. Permanent residency requires separate process (3-yr work permit history typically).
thailand.strategy: Apply LTR Wealthy Global Citizen at FIRE (when net worth > $1M, easily met by year 10-12). Or LTR Wealthy Pensioner if going at $80k+/yr spend in TH. Foreign-sourced income blanket exemption is the killer benefit.
thailand.caveats: LTR visa is the cleanest "FIRE-friendly" visa in Asia. Bumrungrad/BNH private healthcare is excellent.

china.reqs: Held by Taiwan residents/citizens. Card issued at Chinese consulates or via Hong Kong/Macau. 5-year validity, 32 pages. Single-entry version (3-mo max stay) can be upgraded at local Exit-Entry Administration (EEA) office to long-term residence permit. Boss qualifies via Taiwan citizenship — does NOT need a Chinese visa.
china.duration: 5-yr renewable. Stay until permit expiration (effectively unlimited since 2017 reform).
china.pr_pathway: Compatriot Pass holders can renew in mainland China and continue residence indefinitely. Effective long-term residence pathway without needing a Chinese D visa (PR).
china.strategy: Boss's Taiwan citizenship is the key — China is suddenly accessible. Use 台胞證 for indefinite stays in Shanghai or any tier-1 city. Mandarin native. Banking and daily life are friction-free. Visa worry essentially eliminated.
china.caveats: Worldwide income tax after 6 yrs of continuous residency (3-45% progressive). Capital outflow controls remain. VPN required for Western internet. Cross-strait tensions can affect treatment of TW residents — political risk worth tracking.

vietnam.best_visa: DT investor visa OR rolling e-visa
vietnam.reqs: DT (investor): tied to capital invested in a Vietnamese company. Tiered DT1-DT4. DT4 (most retiree-friendly) requires capital deployment, 1-yr validity renewable. E-visa: 90 days, multiple-entry, easily extended (one extension typical). Border runs work but informal.
vietnam.cost: E-visa: $25 USD · DT investor: variable + business setup
vietnam.duration: E-visa 90 days renewable · DT4 1 yr · DT2 5 yrs (for $3M+ investment)
vietnam.pr_pathway: Temporary Residence Card (TRC) 1-3 yrs via investment / marriage / work. Permanent Residence Card (PRC) requires 3 yrs continuous TRC residency.
vietnam.strategy: Two paths. Realistic FIRE path: rolling e-visa with extensions (~18 months feasible before border run needed). Stable path: DT investor visa via setting up a small Vietnamese entity (consulting/services) with $50-100k seed capital — gives you a TRC.
vietnam.caveats: No retirement visa is a structural risk — policy could tighten. Worldwide income tax at 5-35% if resident (>183 days). New Golden Visa proposed (2025 reform) but not yet operational at scale.

philippines.reqs: Age 50+: $10k bank deposit (with $800+/mo pension proof, $1k for dependents) OR $20k deposit (no pension). Age 40-49: $25k deposit (with pension) or $50k (no pension). Boss FIREs at 54 → 50+ tier. Apostilled US police clearance + medical certificate.
philippines.duration: Indefinite stay, multi-entry, no minimum stay required
philippines.pr_pathway: SRRV IS permanent residency. Renewable indefinitely. Convertible to citizenship after 10 yrs continuous residence.
philippines.strategy: Strongest visa fit in the lineup. Process is 20-30 working days. Deposit returns to you when you exit. The SRRV is what the Philippines designed FOR fire-retirees like Boss.
philippines.caveats: Deposit must come via SWIFT from a bank in your name OUTSIDE the Philippines (no local transfers, no cash). Worldwide income tax for residents (0-35%), but cost of living is so low net outflow is minimal.

indonesia.reqs: E33F (age 55+): $3,000/mo passive income proof + sponsor + domestic helper + 12-mo property lease. E33E "Silver Hair" (age 60+): $3k/mo income + $2k bank balance, no sponsor/helper needed, 5-yr validity. Boss FIREs Bali at 55 → exact fit for E33F. At age 60 → upgrade to Silver Hair.
indonesia.pr_pathway: KITAP (permanent residence) after 5 yrs continuous KITAS. Or Golden Visa: IDR 38B (~$2.4M USD) for 5-yr residence; IDR 76B (~$4.8M) for 10-yr.
indonesia.strategy: Time FIRE departure for age 55 EXACTLY to drop into the E33F lane. Renew yearly for 5 yrs → upgrade to E33E Silver Hair (no sponsor) at 60 → KITAP (PR) after another 5 yrs of KITAS continuity at age ~65.
indonesia.caveats: Cannot work, run a business, or earn local income on KITAS (rental income from property is restricted). 12-month lease requirement means commit to one location upfront. BIMC private hospital in Kuta is the best on-island; serious care = Singapore evac.

japan.best_visa: Designated Activities OR Business Manager (Oct 2025 reform) OR Hybrid <180 days
japan.reqs: (1) DESIGNATED ACTIVITIES: case-by-case discretion for wealthy retirees, no published threshold (~$10M+ assets anecdotally), Japan-based bank, Japanese-speaking immigration lawyer needed. (2) BUSINESS MANAGER (Oct 16, 2025 reform — 6× harder): minimum capital ¥30M (~$200k USD, was ¥5M), MUST hire ≥1 full-time Japanese national OR Permanent Resident OR Spouse/Child of Japanese OR Long-Term Resident, applicant or hired employee must hold JLPT N2 / CEFR B2 Japanese, 3+ yrs management experience OR master's/doctoral degree, business plan must be feasibility-reviewed by certified SME advisor (中小企業診断士) / tax accountant / CPA. (3) HSP (Highly Skilled Professional): points-based 70+ pts via salary, education, age, language. Requires Japan employer or self-sponsored business. (4) HYBRID <180 DAYS: see slide — visa-free tourist (90 days × 2 with re-entry), avoid tax residency.
japan.cost: Designated Activities ~$30 + lawyer ($5-15k) · Business Manager ~$200k capital + ¥1M annual employee salary · HSP ~$30
japan.duration: 6 mo to 5 yrs · case-by-case at renewal
japan.pr_pathway: 10 yrs continuous residency standard. HSP holders: 3 yrs (70 pts) or 1 yr (80 pts).
japan.strategy: Three viable paths post Oct 2025: (A) DESIGNATED ACTIVITIES — apply with $10M+ asset dossier and immigration lawyer (~$10-15k legal fees). Discretionary but highest-quality outcome. (B) BUSINESS MANAGER (the new hard mode) — set up a kabushiki-kaisha (KK), inject ¥30M capital, hire one Japanese citizen/PR full-time (typical salary ¥3-5M/yr ≈ $20-33k), get the JLPT N2 OR hire someone who has it, get an SME advisor to bless the business plan. Total Year 1 cost: ~$200k capital + $25-35k salary + $10k professional fees = $235-245k upfront. The visa is then renewable as long as the business runs. (C) HYBRID — see Hybrid Plan slide. Stay <180 days/yr in Japan (visa-free 90-day stints), avoid tax residency, base in Taiwan or another country. Best ROI for Boss's profile.
japan.caveats: Boss's hunch was right — Japan is the hardest by far. The Oct 2025 Business Manager reform raised the bar 6× overnight, which is why hybrid is the smart move unless you genuinely want to operate a Japanese business. NHI is excellent and cheap once you're in — but tax-residency triggers on 1+ yr stay (worldwide income, 5-45% + 10% local).

singapore.reqs: GIP (PR by investment): S$10M (~$7.5M USD) into a Singapore business. ONE Pass: for top earners ($30k+/mo employed, or VC-backed founder, or top creative/scientific talent) — requires active employment or business. LTVP (Long-Term Visit Pass): only via family — Singapore citizen/PR spouse, parent, or child as sponsor.
singapore.cost: GIP application + S$10M investment commitment · ONE Pass S$300 USD
singapore.duration: GIP → PR (permanent) · ONE Pass 5 yrs renewable
singapore.pr_pathway: GIP is PR from day 1. 2-yr-PR → citizenship eligible (but Singapore disallows dual citizenship — Boss would have to renounce US).
singapore.strategy: For a $1M-$3M FIRE retiree, Singapore is structurally inaccessible. GIP threshold is 7-10× Boss's likely FIRE net worth. Without family ties or active business, the only realistic Singapore exposure is rolling 90-day social visit passes — fine for vacations, not for retirement.
singapore.caveats: Singapore is not a viable retirement landing zone for Boss's asset profile. The deck and workbook still include it for completeness, but treat the 19-yr-to-FIRE math as theoretical: even with $3M FIRE number, you'd be S$7M short of GIP. Use Singapore as a base for Asia travel, not residency.
